codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of skip_cache, ignore_pools, and silent query params. Clean TDD workflow: tests written first, then implementation. Refactored parse_price_params by extracting _parse_block, _parse_amount, and _parse_bool_with_default helpers, reducing complexity. All 10 expectedBehavior items are covered by tests. The kwargs-based forwarding in _fetch_price correctly conditionally passes params to ypricemagic's get_price(). Cache bypass logic is correct: skip_cache=True skips cache read but still writes results. Backwards compatibility is maintained — omitting new params preserves all existing behavior. 42 new tests, mypy clean, ruff clean.

issues.description: kwargs dict approach (dict[str, Any]) for get_price call loses static type safety compared to explicit keyword arguments. Since ypricemagic is a dynamic external dep with lazy imports, this is pragmatic but means mypy can't verify the forwarded kwargs match get_price's actual signature. Tests mitigate this by asserting exact call args.

Subtle behavior change: amount=None was previously passed explicitly to get_price as a keyword argument. Now it is omitted entirely when None (only added to kwargs dict when not None). This should be semantically equivalent for Python default params, but is a functional change beyond the feature scope.

openapi.json is stale — lists only 3 /price params (token, block, amount) but endpoint now accepts 6 (skip_cache, ignore_pools, silent were added). This file appears to be a static export and may need regeneration.
The inline HTML UI (INDEX_HTML) does not expose the new skip_cache, ignore_pools, or silent params in its form. Not required by the feature spec, but means the browser UI and API are out of sync.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used --no-verify to bypass pre-commit hooks during commit due to a .secrets.baseline file issue. The .secrets.baseline file exists on disk but is not tracked by git, causing detect-secrets pre-commit hook to fail. This workaround is not documented anywhere.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker ran git commit with --no-verify after investigating .secrets.baseline. File exists at repo root but 'git ls-files .secrets.baseline' returns empty.

sharedStateObservations.observation: openapi.json in repo root is a static export that becomes stale when endpoints change. No documented convention for when/how to regenerate it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: openapi.json has 3 params for /price (token, block, amount) but the endpoint now accepts 6. No regeneration step in the skill procedure or AGENTS.md.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The inline HTML UI (INDEX_HTML in server.py) is a manually maintained HTML string that must be updated separately when API params change. The feature description focused on API-level changes and did not mention UI, but the UI form is now incomplete relative to the API.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.py INDEX_HTML variable contains form fields for chain, token, block, amount only. New params skip_cache, ignore_pools, silent have no corresponding UI inputs.

summary: Feature passes review. Implementation is clean, well-tested (42 new tests), and follows existing project patterns. All 10 expectedBehavior items are satisfied. The refactoring of parse_price_params into extracted helpers is a good improvement. Non-blocking issues: openapi.json is stale, HTML UI doesn't expose new params, and kwargs-based forwarding trades type safety for flexibility. No bugs or blocking issues found.
